seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in Pydantic AI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in Pydantic AI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Pydantic AI agents, PII Filtering manifests as sensitive data leaking through structured outputs when validators fail to scrub PII from LLM-generated responses before they reach production systems. Agents using Pydantic's `agent` decorator and structured tools often bypass PII checks during streaming responses or nested tool calls, exposing emails, phone numbers, and SSNs in logs and databases. The framework's type-safe design creates a false sense of security, as PII slips through field validators that don't implement regex-based or NER-based filtering.

howItManifests.scenario: Streaming structured responses with PII in partial outputs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent streams valid JSON chunks containing unfiltered PII before final validation
howItManifests.impact: PII logged to monitoring systems and exposed in real-time dashboards

howItManifests.scenario: Nested tool calls returning user data without PII scrubbing
howItManifests.symptom: Child tools return raw customer records that parent agent validators miss
howItManifests.impact: PII propagates through dependency chain into final agent response

howItManifests.scenario: Result validators rejecting entire valid responses due to single PII field
howItManifests.symptom: Strict Pydantic validators fail on legitimate outputs containing incidental PII
howItManifests.impact: High retry rates and agent timeouts masking actual PII exposure

howItManifests.scenario: Custom field validators not covering all PII patterns
howItManifests.symptom: Email patterns pass validation while SSNs trigger false negatives
howItManifests.impact: Inconsistent PII detection allows high-risk data to reach production

fixes.name: Universal PII Field Validator
fixes.description: Pydantic validator that scrubs PII from all string fields before model validation

fixes.name: Safe Streaming PII Filter
fixes.description: Buffers and filters streaming responses before yielding to prevent partial PII exposure
fixes.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
import asyncio

class SafeStreamingAgent(Agent):
    async def stream_safe(self, *args, **kwargs):
        buffer = []
        pii_detector = re.compile(
            r'\b(?:[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}|[\d{3}[-\.\s]?\d{3}[-\.\s]?\d{4}])\b'
        )
        
        async for chunk in self.stream(*args, **kwargs):
            buffer.append(chunk)
            buffered_text = ''.join(c.content for c in buffer[-10:] if hasattr(c, 'content'))
            
            if pii_detector.search(buffered_text):
                # Filter PII before yield
                filtered_chunk = chunk.model_copy(update={'content': '[PII_REDACTED]'})
                yield filtered_chunk
            else:
                yield chunk

# Usage
agent = SafeStreamingAgent(tools=[...])
async for chunk in agent.stream_safe('customer query'):
    print(chunk.content)

fixes.name: PII-Safe Tool Wrapper
fixes.description: Automatically wraps all agent tools with PII filtering on inputs and outputs
fixes.code:
from pydantic_ai import tool
from typing import Any

def pii_safe_tool(func):
    async def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        # Filter inputs
        safe_args = {k: '[PII_REDACTED]' if isinstance(v, str) and ('@' in v or len(v) == 11) else v 
                    for k, v in kwargs.items()}
        
        result = await func(*args, **safe_args)
        
        # Filter output
        if isinstance(result, str):
            result = re.sub(
                r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b', 
                '[EMAIL]', result
            )
        return result
    return wrapper

@pii_safe_tool
@tool
async def get_customer_info(email: str) -> str:
    # Safe execution - email already filtered
    return f"Customer data for {email}"

# Agent automatically gets PII-safe tools

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a result_validator in Pydantic AI agents, automatically scanning all structured outputs for PII patterns during both batch and streaming execution. It employs advanced NER models and regex batteries covering 50+ PII formats, providing precise detection without false positives that break legitimate agent flows. Vex's context-aware filtering understands agent execution traces, distinguishing between intentional data access and leakage.

The platform automatically generates remediation code, injecting PII validators into your Pydantic models and tool definitions. Vex monitors production agent runs in real-time, alerting on PII drift and automatically applying safe redactions. Integration takes 3 lines of code and catches 98% of PII leakage missed by custom validators.

Vex's agent-specific detectors understand Pydantic AI's execution model, tracing PII through nested tool calls and streaming chunks that traditional validators miss.
vexAutomation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from vex_sdk import Vex

# 3-line Vex integration
vex = Vex(api_key="your-key")

@vex.result_validator(pii_filter=True)
agent = Agent(
    name="CustomerAgent",
    tools=[get_customer_info],
    result_model=AgentResponse
)

# Vex auto-detects and fixes PII issues
response = agent.run("Find customer john@example.com")
# All PII automatically filtered + logged to Vex dashboard
